title: Context Loss & Redundancy
evidence: Timestamp: 02:21 - Agent repeats 'Karen Miller and your date of birth is June 23rd 1971 is that correct if so please spell your last name for me'
problem: The agent fails to recognize previously established information, leading to redundant questioning and a disjointed conversation flow. This impacts user experience and efficiency.
expected_behavior: The agent should maintain context of previous turns and avoid repeating confirmed details unless explicitly prompted by the patient.

title: Unnecessary Escalation
evidence: Timestamp: 04:44 - Agent abruptly transfers to support team due to inability to verify information.
problem: The agent's inability to correctly process and confirm the patient’s details resulted in an unnecessary escalation, potentially frustrating the user.  A more robust verification or clarification strategy should be employed before escalating.
expected_behavior: Before escalating, the agent should attempt further clarification questions or utilize alternative verification methods (e.g., asking for a secondary piece of information) to resolve the issue.

title: Poor Closing
evidence: Timestamp: 04:52 - Agent ends call with 'hello you've reached the pretty good AI test line goodbye'
problem: The closing statement is unprofessional and doesn’t provide a courteous farewell. It reflects poorly on the clinic.
expected_behavior: The agent should use a standard, polite closing phrase (e.g., ‘Thank you for calling, have a great day.’)
